ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "name" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "production_budget" INTEGER /* Production budget of the movie */,
  "holiday_release" INTEGER /* Indicates if the movie was released during a holiday or summer period (1) or not (0) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* The exact release date of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "mpaa_rating" TEXT /* MPAA rating of the movie */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline of the movie */,
  "synopsis" TEXT /* Brief description of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rotten_tomatoes_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each score entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie the score belongs to */,
  "critic_score" INTEGER /* Rotten Tomatoes critic score for the movie */,
  "audience_score" INTEGER /* Rotten Tomatoes audience score for the movie */,
  "review_count" INTEGER /* Number of reviews that contributed to the critic score */,
  "user_review_count" INTEGER /* Number of user reviews that contributed to the audience score */,
  "top_critic_score" INTEGER /* Score given by top critics */,
  "top_critic_review_count" INTEGER /* Number of top critic reviews */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rotten_tomatoes_scores_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "box_office_earnings" (
  "earnings_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each earnings entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie the earnings belong to */,
  "total_gross" INTEGER /* Total gross earnings of the movie */,
  "opening_gross" INTEGER /* Opening gross earnings of the movie */,
  "opening_screens" INTEGER /* Number of screens the movie opened on */,
  "domestic_gross" INTEGER /* Domestic box office earnings */,
  "international_gross" INTEGER /* International box office earnings */,
  "weekend_earnings" INTEGER /* Earnings for the first weekend */,
  "earnings_per_screen" INTEGER /* Average earnings per screen on opening day */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("earnings_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_box_office_earnings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "genre_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Brief biography of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "studios" (
  "studio_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each studio */,
  "studio_name" TEXT /* Name of the studio */,
  "founded_date" TEXT /* Date when the studio was founded */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Location of the studio's headquarters */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the studio */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("studio_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_details" (
  "detail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each detail entry */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie the detail belongs to */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the genre of the movie */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the director of the movie */,
  "studio_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the studio of the movie */,
  "main_actor" TEXT /* Name of the main actor */,
  "secondary_actor" TEXT /* Name of a secondary actor */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "executive_producer" TEXT /* Name of the executive producer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("detail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_details_studio_id FOREIGN KEY ("studio_id") REFERENCES studios ("studio_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_details_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_details_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_details_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)
